title: Neural mechanisms of psychedelic visual imagery.

assets.abstract: Visual alterations under classic psychedelics can include rich phenomenological accounts of eyes-closed imagery. Preclinical evidence suggests agonism of the 5-HT2A receptor may reduce synaptic gain to produce psychedelic-induced imagery. However, this has not been investigated in humans. To infer the directed connectivity changes to visual connectivity underlying psychedelic visual imagery in healthy adults, a double-blind, randomised, placebo-controlled, cross-over study was performed, and dyn

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.
